title: Bread in the Wilderness
subtitle: Christ has compassion on the four thousand who would not leave Him

stillness: He went up the mountain to lift His hearers toward the things above.
He sat there to show that rest is to be sought nowhere but in heaven.
Sit with Him a while, and seek your rest only there.
gospel: Then Jesus went up into a mountain, into a desert place, and sat down there.
And great multitudes came to Him, from many cities and towns and villages,
bringing with them the mute, the blind, the lame, the feeble, and many others.
And they cast them down at His feet, and He healed them.
So the crowds marveled, seeing the mute speak, the lame walk, the blind see;
and they glorified the God of Israel.
Now when the crowd was great and had nothing to eat,
Jesus called His disciples and said: I have compassion on the multitude;
for behold, three days now they have remained with Me, and have nothing to eat.
And if I send them away fasting to their homes, they will faint on the way.
His disciples answered: How can anyone fill these with bread here in the wilderness?
He asked them: How many loaves have you? They said: Seven, and a few little fishes.
And He commanded the multitude to sit down upon the ground.
behold: Behold the blessed feet of Jesus, where sins are washed away and diseases are cured.
See the crowds carry their mute, their blind, their lame, their feeble,
and cast them down at His feet, showing their faith not in word only but in deed.
He healed them by a word alone — He who by a word alone could make the whole world out of nothing.
See them so taken by Him that, as if carried out of themselves, they cared nothing for the lack of food,
and for three days did not return to their own homes,
remaining with Christ in the desert place, under the open sky, and without food.
Yet they could not fail for want of nourishment, because they were with Jesus the Savior.
consider: Behold a word of wondrous sweetness and love: I have compassion on the multitude.
It proceeds from the inmost marrow of His heart, and reaches by its power into our inmost depths.
For there is no other who so pities our miseries as our Maker, whose mercies are over all His works.
He has compassion as true man; He feeds them as God.
First Christ took away the weakness of the sick, and afterward offered food to the healed:
for first sins must be removed, and then the soul nourished with the words of God.
Such is the power of the Creator, that if He forsook His creature, it would utterly fail.
The disciples spoke from a weak thought, not yet believing His power despite the former miracles.
Seven loaves — as if nothing against so great a multitude — and a few little fishes, not many, nor large.
Yet from so small a food so great a crowd was satisfied, by the multiplying of the bread in the hands of Christ.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, have mercy upon the multitude:
upon the penitent, and the just who are beginning, and those advancing, the perfect and the contemplative,
who through a three days' contrition, confession, and satisfaction,
through victory over the world, the flesh, and the devil,
through the bodily, the imaginary, and the spiritual vision,
Refresh the first by watchful care, caution, and holy fear and desire.
Refresh the second by the spirit of fear, of piety, of knowledge, of fortitude, of counsel, of understanding, and of wisdom.
Refresh the third with the dowries of soul and body —
in present hope, which shall overflow in seven baskets in the blessedness to come. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Ecce verbum miræ dulcedinis et amoris, procedens ex intimis cordis medullis, et pertingens suo vigore usque ad intima nostra.
latinJewel.english: Behold a word of wondrous sweetness and love, proceeding from the inmost marrow of the heart, and reaching by its power into our inmost depths.
